title: LDAP Injection
principle: Never concatenate user input into an LDAP filter or DN. Escape it with the library's filter-escaping function (or a parameterized search API) so characters like ( ) * \ and NUL can't change the query's meaning. Validate the input shape first where you can.

snippets.secure:
from ldap3.utils.conv import escape_filter_chars
conn.search(base, f"(uid={escape_filter_chars(username)})")
snippets.note: escape_filter_chars neutralizes ( ) * \ and NUL. For DNs use escape_rdn. python-ldap exposes ldap.filter.escape_filter_chars equivalently.

snippets.library: Spring LDAP filters or the OWASP ESAPI encoder
snippets.vulnerable:
String f = "(uid=" + username + ")";
ctx.search(base, f, controls);
snippets.secure:
Filter f = new EqualsFilter("uid", username); // Spring LDAP escapes the value
ctx.search(base, f.encode(), controls);
snippets.note: Spring LDAP's Filter classes encode values for you. With raw JNDI, escape the value per RFC 4515 (or ESAPI's encodeForLDAP) before building the string.

snippets.library: System.DirectoryServices with an escaped filter value
snippets.secure:
string safe = username.Replace("\\", "\\5c").Replace("*", "\\2a").Replace("(", "\\28").Replace(")", "\\29").Replace("\0", "\\00");
var s = new DirectorySearcher(entry) { Filter = $"(uid={safe})" };
snippets.note: Escape ( ) * \ and NUL to their \XX hex forms per RFC 4515 before interpolating. Prefer a helper so every search path is covered.
go-ldap.EscapeFilter handles the RFC 4515 special characters. Build the filter around the escaped value, never the raw one.

references.title: OWASP LDAP Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
